name: Broadway Black Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups dried black beans, washed and picked over for stones
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the beans and cover with water, then soak overnight.
2. Drain the beans before placing them in a large stock pot and covering with cold water.
3. Bring to a boil, then lower the temperature to low and cover.
4. Simmer for 2 1/2 to 3 hours or until the beans are very tender. Do not add salt, as it makes the beans tough.
5. If using a slow cooker, cook the beans overnight or all day on low.
6. In a large skillet, heat the oil of your choice and sauté onion, cilantro, garlic, cumin, coriander, oregano, chili powder, and red chilies until the onion is tender.
7. Stir in tomatoes, pepper, chicken broth, and garlic to taste. Cook for 5 minutes over medium heat and set aside.
8. Drain the beans and puree them. Return to the stock pot and add the contents of the skillet, stirring well.
9. Turn the heat to medium and cook the soup until heated through. Add additional broth if needed.
10. Stir in brandy or sherry. Serve the soup in bowls and garnish with freshly grated Parmesan cheese and cilantro.
11. Optional: Add a tablespoon of sour cream on top of each bowl or 1 tablespoon of Guacamole Crudo.
